messages.content:
What was observed
- 192.168.0.12 could not reach 172.16.0.12 (25/25 pings lost).

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Missing IP routes between the two LANs prevented forwarding. Interfaces were already up and ACLs allowed traffic once checked.

Changes made
- Added static route on R1: 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.2.
- Added static route on R2: 192.168.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.1.
- Attempted to create explicit ICMP ACL entries via REST; REST calls returned 400 errors, but existing ACLs were validated and already permit traffic (permit ip any any).
- Verified both R1 and R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 interfaces are enabled.

Verification
- Passed.
  - R1 -> 172.16.0.12 (source 192.168.0.1) ping: 100% success (5/5).
  - R2 -> 192.168.0.12 ping: 100% success (5/5).
  - R1 and R2 LAN interfaces: admin up / oper up, no errors.
  - Running-configs show the added static routes.
  - ACLs on both routers contain permit ip any any, allowing ICMP.

Remaining issues
- None identified. Network reachability restored.
